confirmed_note: Verified 2026-07-19. This clip is NOT locked-off (unlike 1.mp4/3.mp4) -- a real, correlated, whole-frame camera motion of up to ~45-68px was found and confirmed with 3 independent corner points moving together (see PROGRESS.md and reference_video4_calibration.py docstring for the full investigation, including a diagnostic-script bug in the first pass -- a too-narrow scan window that clipped and missed the horizontal component entirely -- that was caught and fixed before this conclusion was reached). Two long, stable, POSITION-MATCHED windows exist: frames 0-420 (BL numeric reads 188.0/187.5 at frames 0/300) and frames 740-1240 (BL reads 189.0/188.5 at frames 900/1200) -- these 4 independent readings cluster within ~1.5px of each other, confirming segments A and C are the same camera position, not two separate calibrations. This single homography, calibrated from frame 900, is used for BOTH windows; frames 421-739 and 1241-1543 (the two ramps, including two ~30-frame turning points and a ~44-frame end tail, none long/stable enough on their own to warrant independent calibration per this project's verification standard) are excluded from confident court-line overlay rendering. Rendered overlay visually confirmed tight at all 4 corners on all 4 checked frames (0, 300, 900, 1200 -- calib_check_frame*.jpg in cv_pipeline/scratch_output/tennis4/calib_verify/manifest_frames/), and separately confirmed VISIBLY MISALIGNED at frame 500 (inside the excluded dip) -- see calib_check_frame500.jpg -- directly validating the exclusion decision, not just asserting it from the numeric scan.
